title: Amend T033 widget spec: cross-process freshness (WidgetCenter reload) + read-only container
description: Source: Axiom skills/axiom-data (grdb-app-groups section 7 — conceptually applies to any shared-SQLite setup including SwiftData) + skills/axiom-integration WidgetKit guidance: a reader process NEVER sees another process's writes automatically; the writing process must nudge the widget via WidgetCenter.shared.reloadTimelines(ofKind:) after saves, and the widget should treat its container as read-only per timeline fetch. Evidence in Basket: .harness/tasks/T033.md says only 'Keep the timeline refresh cadence sensible (re-read on a reasonable interval / on app writes)' — no mechanism named, and T033's scope reads widget-side-only, but the reload calls belong APP-side: after context.save() in BasketApp's scenePhase-background flush, after add/check-off/clear mutations, and in AddToBasketIntent.perform() (Siri-add while the widget is on screen is the headline staleness case). Without this, the shipped widget shows stale data for arbitrary periods and the cold builder has no instruction to prevent it. Proposed: amend T033's spec (## Do + ## Done when + scope, which must grow to cover the app-side files) BEFORE the loop builds it — a .harness/tasks/ + TASKS.json scope edit, so per CLAUDE.md do it interactively or as a needs-human task. Also fold in: verify the widget target's required-reason API usage (App Group UserDefaults, file timestamps) is covered by PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy, per the security agent's T033 note. Value: HIGH (prevents shipping a stale-by-design widget). Cost: small.
